skill.name: amap-lbs-skill
skill.description: 高德地图综合服务，支持POI搜索、路径规划、旅游规划、周边搜索和热力图数据可视化
skill.summary: Search locations, plan routes, and create travel itineraries using the Amap Web Service API for geographic data in China.
skill.author: AMap-Web

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported 316 potential issues, but after evaluation nearly all are false positives. JavaScript template literals (backticks in console.log) were misidentified as shell command execution. Coordinate strings and Chinese text were misclassified as cryptographic algorithms and obfuscated content. The skill legitimately reads an API key from environment variables, makes HTTP requests to the official Amap REST API (restapi.amap.com), and reads/writes a local config.json file. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unauthorized behavior was found. This is a standard map service API wrapper.

medium_findings.title: Shell backtick execution in index.js (FALSE POSITIVE)
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 14 backtick usages in index.js as shell command execution. All are JavaScript template literal strings used in console.log() statements. No actual shell execution occurs. Examples: console.log(`Total results: ${count}`) at line 106, console.log(`${index + 1}. ${poi.name}`) at lines 400-403.

medium_findings.title: Shell backtick execution in Markdown files (FALSE POSITIVE)
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick usage in README.md, SKILL.md as shell execution. These are markdown code blocks showing example shell commands for documentation purposes. They are not executed by the skill.

medium_findings.title: Path traversal in require statements (FALSE POSITIVE)
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged '../index' in require() calls as path traversal sequences. These are standard Node.js relative module imports (require('../index') loads the parent directory's index.js module). Not a security vulnerability.

medium_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged over 50 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithms'. None of these are actual crypto usage. The scanner confused coordinate strings (e.g., 116.397428,39.90923), package.json descriptions, and Chinese text with cryptographic references. No crypto module is imported or used anywhere in the codebase.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API key (LEGITIMATE)
low_findings.description: The skill reads process.env.AMAP_KEY and process.env.AMAP_WEBSERVICE_KEY to obtain the Amap Web Service API key. This is standard credential management for API-based skills. The key is sent to the official Amap API endpoint (restapi.amap.com) for authentication. No exfiltration or unauthorized transmission occurs.

low_findings.title: Synchronous filesystem operations (LEGITIMATE)
low_findings.description: The skill uses fs.readFileSync and fs.writeFileSync to manage a local config.json file for API key persistence. The file path is fixed to __dirname/config.json with no user-controllable input. This is a standard Node.js CLI pattern and does not introduce path traversal risks.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to Amap API endpoints (LEGITIMATE)
low_findings.description: The skill hardcodes URLs for the official Amap Web Service API (restapi.amap.com). All URLs point to documented, legitimate Amap API endpoints for POI search, direction routing, and geocoding. No requests are made to unknown or suspicious domains.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation - high entropy strings (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Chinese-language content in README.md, SKILL.md, and package.json as high-entropy obfuscated content. Chinese characters have higher byte-level entropy than ASCII text but are legitimate documentation. The skill is authored in Chinese for Chinese-speaking users of the Amap map service.

low_findings.title: Hidden file access in .gitignore (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged .gitignore and .specstory/.gitignore as 'hidden file access' and 'environment file access'. These are standard Git configuration files listing patterns to exclude. The .gitignore contains entries for node_modules, config.json, .env, and log files. No actual file access occurs from reading .gitignore.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local API key persistence in config.json
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads and writes API credentials to config.json in the project directory. While the path is hardcoded and not controllable by user input, storing secrets in a local file is a low-risk pattern. The .gitignore excludes config.json from version control to prevent accidental exposure.

dangerous_patterns.title: API credential sent via URL query parameters
dangerous_patterns.description: The Amap API key is sent as a URL query parameter (?key=...) in HTTP GET requests. This is the standard authentication method for the Amap Web Service API and is used over HTTPS. The appname parameter is also appended as required by Amap's terms of service.

content.user_title: Search places and plan routes with Amap map service
content.value_statement: You need a map API wrapper to search locations and plan routes across China. This skill connects to the Amap Web Service API to find points of interest and calculate driving, walking, cycling, and transit directions with shareable map links.
actual_capabilities: Search for points of interest by keyword, city, and category type
Plan routes for walking, driving, cycling, and public transit
Create multi-stop travel itineraries with route visualization links
Search nearby locations by coordinates and radius
Read API credentials from environment variables or local config file
Generate shareable map visualization links for routes and points
limitations: Requires an active Amap Web Service API key to function
Limited to geographic areas covered by Amap (primarily China)
API rate limits apply based on your Amap service plan tier
Does not support real-time traffic, GPS tracking, or offline maps

use_cases.title: Travel itinerary planning
use_cases.description: A traveler planning a visit to Beijing can search for attractions, restaurants, and hotels and get walking routes between them with a shareable map link.

use_cases.title: Delivery route calculation
use_cases.description: A logistics coordinator needs to calculate driving distances and routes between multiple delivery points in a Chinese city to estimate travel times.

use_cases.title: Local business research
use_cases.description: A business analyst searching for competitors or partners by category in a specific city can search and list POI data with addresses, phone numbers, and coordinates.

prompt_templates.title: Simple location search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for coffee shops in Shanghai.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a quick list of places in a city

prompt_templates.title: Route between two places
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a walking route from Tiananmen Square to the Forbidden City in Beijing.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs walking directions between two landmarks

prompt_templates.title: Travel itinerary with multiple interests
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 1-day travel itinerary for Hangzhou including scenic spots, restaurants, and tea houses with walking routes between them.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a complete day trip plan with routes

prompt_templates.title: Nearby search with coordinates
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for hotels near 116.397428,39.90923 within 1000 meters and show their names, addresses, and distances.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to find places near a specific coordinate point

output_examples.input: Find hotels near the Bund in Shanghai.
output: I searched for hotels near the Bund, Shanghai and found 8 results within 1 km.
Top matches include: The Peninsula Shanghai, and 7 other hotels.
A map link has been generated showing all hotel locations.

output_examples.input: Plan a driving route from Beijing Capital Airport (116.585556,40.080278) to Tiananmen Square (116.397428,39.90923).
output: The driving route from Beijing Capital Airport to Tiananmen Square is approximately 28 km.
Estimated travel time is about 35 minutes without traffic.
A map visualization link has been generated for this route.

output_examples.input: Search for museums in Xi'an and show the top 5 results.
output: I found 12 museums in Xi'an. Here are the top 5:
1. Shaanxi History Museum - Address: 91 Xiaozhai East Road
2. Xi'an Museum - Address: 72 Youyi West Road
3. Terracotta Warriors Museum - Address: Lintong District
4. Xi'an Art Museum - Address: 1 Yanta South Road
5. Shaanxi Natural History Museum - Address: 88 Chang'an South Road
Each listing includes address, phone number, and coordinates.

best_practices: Set your Amap Web Service Key as an environment variable (AMAP_WEBSERVICE_KEY) before using any search or routing functions for secure credential management.
Use specific city names and keywords to get the most relevant POI search results and avoid excessive API calls.
Verify coordinate order is longitude first, then latitude (standard for the Amap API) when providing location parameters.
anti_patterns: Do not commit config.json with your API key to version control. Use environment variables instead and verify config.json is in .gitignore.
Do not ignore API rate limits. Excessive calls may result in service suspension. Cache results when possible.
Do not use this skill for real-time navigation or emergency routing. It provides route estimates only and is not a GPS navigation tool.

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a free Amap Web Service API key from the Amap Open Platform at https://lbs.amap.com. Set it as the AMAP_WEBSERVICE_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Is this skill limited to Chinese locations?
faq.answer: Yes, Amap primarily covers China. Search results and route data outside China may not be available or accurate.

faq.question: How do I set my API key?
faq.answer: Set the AMAP_WEBSERVICE_KEY environment variable. The skill also checks the AMAP_KEY variable as a fallback and can store the key in a local config.json file.

faq.question: What types of route planning are supported?
faq.answer: Walking, driving, cycling, and public transit routes are all supported. You can specify the route type when making a request.

faq.question: Does this skill store my search history?
faq.answer: Search data is not stored or transmitted to third parties. Only the Amap API receives your search queries for processing. The config.json file stores only your API key.

faq.question: Is there a cost to use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free and open source under the MIT license. Amap offers free daily API call quotas, with paid plans available for higher usage volumes.
